title: How It Works
description: Upload lab PDFs, review extracted values, explore your timeline, and export a visit brief — four steps to organized lab history.

seo.title: How MedLens+ Works — 4 Steps to a Lab Timeline
seo.description: Upload lab PDFs, see extracted values on a timeline, compare across visits, and export a brief before your next appointment — the full four-step workflow.

sections.heading: Step 1: Upload your reports
paragraphs: Add lab reports as PDFs or images from any laboratory — CBC, liver and kidney panels, lipid profiles, HbA1c, thyroid tests, and key vitamins.
Your originals stay secure. We extract marker names, values, units, and reference ranges exactly as printed on each document.

sections.heading: Step 2: Review what we extracted
paragraphs: After processing, check the structured data pulled from each report. Values are matched to the reference ranges on your document — not generic web tables.
If a field looks off or a panel seems incomplete, note it before it enters your timeline. Good source data makes better longitudinal views.

sections.heading: Step 3: Explore your timeline
paragraphs: Values land on a chronological timeline so you can see how markers shift across visits. Tap LDL and watch it move from 118 to 142 over eighteen months.
When reports come from different labs, you'll see a cross-lab caution. The timeline shows what changed; your clinician explains why.

sections.heading: Step 4: Prep for your visit
paragraphs: Export a brief summarizing recent shifts, flagged values relative to your report ranges, and a few discussion topics worth raising.
Your provider still drives medical decisions. The brief just means you arrive with context instead of a folder of unsorted PDFs.

sections.heading: Built for ongoing care
paragraphs: MedLens+ fits recurring lab work — annual checkups, chronic condition monitoring, specialist follow-ups, caregiver coordination. Each new upload extends your timeline automatically.
Over time, your organized history becomes the thing you reach for before every clinical conversation.
